source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A certain mass of ideal gas is sealed within a beverage bottle. At a temperature of $t=27^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$, the pressure is $p= 1.050 \times 10^{5}\mathrm{{Pa}}$.

1. What is the gas pressure when the temperature is $t^{\prime}=37^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$?
2. Maintaining the temperature constant, the gas is compressed until its pressure is the same as in (1); what is the ratio of the new gas volume to the original volume?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate some upstream expectation of image use
image_manifest shows 1 existing PNG asset with no registry issues and registry_passed=true

question_text itself states all relevant quantities (initial temperature, initial pressure, new temperature, compression condition) and does not explicitly reference any visual element, so dependence on the image is not strongly evidenced
multi_step: The problem has two linked sub-questions: first compute pressure after temperature change, then use that result to infer a volume ratio under constant temperature compression
Solving requires combining at least two gas-law relations or equivalent proportional reasoning across different conditions
The second part depends on the result or target condition established in part (1), which increases process depth beyond a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text provides specific numerical outputs
The task is a standard ideal-gas calculation with checkable formulas and units, so both final values can be independently verified
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
high multi-step score and high verifiability score indicate strong candidate quality
image-dependency metadata has some conflict flags, but the core problem semantics remain recoverable from the available text and answer
